column1: # lesson<TAB>reason — lessons requiring real-VM/host access; iximiuz Labs only.
# The local runner confines lesson scripts to the kind node container
# (SECURITY.md, commit 292ebb2), so these are structurally unofferable locally.
# A row here means: this lesson's tasks run on iximiuz's disposable VMs and
# nowhere else. The reason string is shown to the user by the TUI, the local
# runner, and /catalog — write it as a sentence completing "…because it …".
# Not lesson frontmatter: `labctl content push` 400s on unknown keys
# (iximiuz/README.md). This file is never pushed (--dir courses/kubelings).
# Validated by docs/scripts/gen-catalog.py: every row must name a real lesson
# that has tasks, and must carry a reason.

column2: it writes an EncryptionConfiguration, patches the kube-apiserver static pod manifest, and reads raw rows out of etcd
it installs an audit policy file and adds kube-apiserver flags on the control-plane node
it runs kubeadm certs renew as root and restarts the control-plane static pods
it snapshots and restores etcd's on-disk data directory and stops the control plane to do it
it loads an eBPF probe into the node kernel and installs a privileged Falco DaemonSet, host-level and outside the kubectl sandbox
it runs kubeadm reset and kubeadm join as root on a worker node to tear it out of the cluster and re-bootstrap it
it kills a control-plane static pod on the node to force a scheduler leader-election failover
it plants a broken systemd drop-in and restarts the kubelet on a worker, diagnosed with journalctl and systemctl on the host
it overrides the containerd systemd unit to break the CRI on a worker and recovers it with crictl and systemctl
it injects a bad flag into the kube-apiserver static-pod manifest to crash-loop the control plane, read back through crictl logs
it drives etcdctl against the live backend to compact, defragment, and disarm a NOSPACE alarm on the control-plane node
it writes a pod manifest into /etc/kubernetes/manifests on a node for the kubelet to run directly
it forges and approves a node CertificateSigningRequest against the cluster signer as root on the control plane
it edits the kubelet and containerd cgroup-driver config on a node and restarts both host services
it moves the node system clock and repairs time synchronisation with timedatectl and chronyd on the host
it loads a kernel module and writes sysctls on a node to restore pod-networking prerequisites the kubectl sandbox can't touch
